sanskrit:
मां हि पार्थ व्यपाश्रित्य येऽपि स्युः पापयोनयः।
स्त्रियो वैश्यास्तथा शूद्रास्तेऽपि यान्ति परां गतिम्॥
transliteration:
māṃ hi pārtha vyapāśritya ye 'pi syuḥ pāpa-yonayaḥ |
striyo vaiśyās tathā śūdrās te 'pi yānti parāṃ gatim ||
literal_meaning: Even those of sinful birth—women, merchants, laborers—taking refuge in Me attain the supreme goal.
contextual_meaning: Krishna dismantles the internalized hierarchy that labels some identities as 'less worthy,' affirming that radical self-acceptance and devotion dissolve shame-based identities.
psychological_state: Cognitive liberation from caste/ gender/ class schemas; activation of secure attachment to the divine inner presence.
trigger_scenario: A first-generation college student from a marginalized background fears they don’t ‘belong’ in elite academic or spiritual spaces; this verse reframes their narrative from deficit to inherent worthiness.
anchor_text: Your birth or past does not disqualify you from the highest love.
